context.projectOverview: Adds a single stack-aware bober-security-auditor role driving one runSecurityAudit() core, consumed by an in-pipeline fail-closed gate that vetoes sprint-passed on critical findings/timeout/parse-failure and a standalone `bober security-audit` CLI with a configurable blocking threshold. Findings reuse the locked ReviewResult shape, persist to .bober/security/, and flow into the priority hub; an opt-in deterministic scanner pre-filter (with slither and semgrep parsers) grounds the LLM judgment. Implements arch-20260712-security-audit-agent-team (ADR-1..6) for users whose generated code manages real customer funds.

completedSprints.title: Security config section, audit types, .bober/security store
completedSprints.summary: SecuritySectionSchema opt-in default-off (incl. scanners: z.array(EvalStrategySchema) default [], timeoutMs default 300000); security-audit-types.ts; security-audit-state.ts.

completedSprints.title: bober-security-auditor agent + runSecurityAudit core + stack knowledge
completedSprints.summary: runSecurityAudit(contract, evaluation|null, projectRoot, config, priors?) — priors?: SecurityFinding[] is the SEAM THIS SPRINT FILLS (currently defaults to [] and renders a priors section into the auditor prompt when non-empty). Fail-closed parse; curator-role read-only tools; persists internally. stack-knowledge.ts; agents/bober-security-auditor.md.

completedSprints.title: Fail-closed SecurityAuditGate in the pipeline
completedSprints.summary: security-gate.ts evaluateSecurityGate (Promise.race time-box vs config.security.timeoutMs, never throws, 5 reasons) + renderSecurityFeedback; pipeline gate additive, byte-identical when disabled. The gate's shared AbortSignal/time-box context matters for this sprint: scanner children must die when the audit window closes.

completedSprints.title: Standalone `bober security-audit` CLI
completedSprints.summary: src/cli/commands/security-audit.ts: registerSecurityAuditCommand, buildAuditDescriptor, DI core runStandaloneSecurityAudit, pure thresholdVerdict (CLI-only standaloneBlockOn); exit 2 on threshold-bucket/parse-failure/throw, else 0. Commit 61e055a. Suite 4004.

contract.title: Implement the scanner pre-filter with slither and semgrep parsers
contract.description: Deterministic ground truth for the LLM auditor: a SecurityScannerPreFilter that runs config.security.scanners commands under the shared audit AbortSignal with per-scanner isolation (missing binary → [], hang → SIGKILL), dedicated parsers mapping slither JSON and semgrep JSON output to SecurityFinding[] (fixture-tested, binaries NOT required in CI), a raw-text fallback for unknown scanners, and wiring of the resulting priors into runSecurityAudit's prompt (ADR-4: absent scanners config = pure LLM pass, zero child processes).

successCriteria.description: The slither parser maps a committed slither --json output fixture to the expected SecurityFinding[] (path, line, severity bucket, vulnClass, description) and the semgrep parser does the same for a committed semgrep --json fixture; both are pure functions with malformed-input tests returning [] (never throwing).

runScannerPreFilter executes each configured scanner command via execa with per-scanner isolation: a missing binary (ENOENT) or nonzero exit yields [] for that scanner while other scanners still run and contribute; the function never rejects.
When the shared AbortSignal fires mid-scan, scanner child processes are killed (SIGKILL/forceKill semantics) and the pre-filter resolves with the findings gathered so far (partial results, no hang).

Parser selection is name/command-based (slither→slither parser, semgrep→semgrep parser, anything else→raw-text-excerpt fallback finding priors), and scanner priors appear in the auditor prompt under a deterministic-ground-truth section with scannerRan:true on the result; with scanners:[] no child process is spawned, scannerRan is false, and the prompt has no priors section (byte-identical to sprint 2 behavior).
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass; ESLint zero errors; full suite green — and no test requires slither or semgrep binaries to be installed (fixtures only).

nonGoals: Do not add parsers beyond slither and semgrep (raw-text fallback covers the rest).
Do not let scanner findings bypass the LLM auditor or directly drive the verdict — they are priors; the audit's review buckets remain the verdict source.
Do not install or vendor scanner binaries; do not add npm dependencies (execa already exists).
Do not change the gate or CLI (they inherit priors through the core).
stopConditions: All five criteria verified; CI-offline property confirmed (suite passes on a machine without slither/semgrep).
contract.definitionOfDone: Operators can configure scanner commands per project; slither/semgrep output arrives as typed, fixture-verified SecurityFinding priors in the auditor prompt under the shared time-box, unknown scanners degrade to raw-text priors, and unconfigured audits remain pure LLM passes with zero child processes.
assumptions: Scanner entries reuse the EvalStrategy shape's command field (config.security.scanners: EvalStrategySchema[] from sprint 1, per ADR-4); the scanner 'name' for parser selection derives from the strategy's name/command string (e.g. command starts with or contains 'slither'/'semgrep').
execa (already a dependency) supports signal-based cancellation/forceKill for sc-5-3; child stdout is bounded (maxBuffer or truncation) so a pathological scanner cannot exhaust memory.

Slither fixture shape: results.detectors[] with elements[].source_mapping (filename_relative, lines[]), impact (High/Medium/Low), check id, description. Semgrep fixture shape: results[] with path, start.line, extra.severity (ERROR/WARNING), check_id, extra.message. Severity mapping: slither High-impact and semgrep ERROR → critical bucket priors; lower severities → important. The generator verifies these shapes against real tool docs/output when writing fixtures and documents the mapping in code.
Priors are prompt-side only: sprint 2's runSecurityAudit already accepts priors?: SecurityFinding[] (or gains it now with a default that keeps existing tests green).
outOfScope: Hub emission (sprint 6), skill/dogfood/docs (sprint 7), any additional scanner parsers, dependency-vulnerability audit wiring (npm audit etc.).
contract.generatorNotes: Anchors may have drifted — re-locate by pattern. (1) src/orchestrator/security-scanners.ts: export runScannerPreFilter({scanners, projectRoot, signal}) plus pure parseSlitherOutput(json: unknown) and parseSemgrepOutput(json: unknown) — parse with defensive narrowing (unknown → shape checks), NEVER trust fixture shape at runtime; malformed → []. Map vulnClass heuristically from check ids (e.g. slither 'reentrancy-*' → leave vulnClass undefined unless a clean mapping exists — vulnClass is optional; do not force wrong classes). (2) execa usage: follow existing execa call sites (fleet spawner, eval strategies) for options style; pass {reject:false} or try/catch per scanner; wire signal via execa's cancelSignal/signal option and forceKillAfterDelay for sc-5-3. Cap stdout (maxBuffer) and truncate raw-text fallback priors to a bounded excerpt (~2000 chars). (3) Fixtures: commit realistic minimal slither/semgrep JSON files under a __fixtures__ dir next to the tests (grep for how medical MedlinePlus fixtures are laid out and follow that convention). (4) Wire into runSecurityAudit: when config.security.scanners is non-empty, call the pre-filter INSIDE the audit path (so the gate's existing Promise.race time-box covers it) before building the prompt; pass an AbortSignal derived from the same timeout — check how sprint 3's gate structured the race and thread the signal (if the gate races an un-abortable promise today, create the AbortController in runSecurityAudit keyed to security.timeoutMs and pass its signal to the pre-filter). (5) For the abort test: use a fake long-running command (node -e with a sleep) rather than a real scanner — keep it fast with a short signal fire.
contract.evaluatorNotes: For sc-5-1: verify fixtures look like REAL tool output (spot-check field names against slither/semgrep documentation — a fixture invented to match the parser is circular); malformed-input tests must include truncated JSON and a valid-JSON-wrong-shape case. For sc-5-2: three-scanner test where the middle one is a nonexistent binary — assert the other two contribute and no rejection. For sc-5-3: fire the AbortSignal after the first scanner completes and assert partial findings + child killed (process no longer running) within a bounded time. For sc-5-4: capture the fake client's prompt with and without scanners configured; assert the priors section is present/absent respectively and scannerRan flag matches; assert zero execa calls with scanners:[] (spy or injected runner). For sc-5-5: grep the new tests for any invocation of real 'slither'/'semgrep' binaries — none allowed. Full suite green.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
